claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I'm asking about my son Alex. He's 18 and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to know a bit more about his current situation.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's still in sixth form doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is 18 and in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels), he may still qualify for Child Benefit.

Can you confirm that you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex, or are you applying for the first time? And is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm looking to claim it - just checking if I'm eligible. And no, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

Do you live with Alex, or if not, are you contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards his upkeep (such as food, clothes, or pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

Although Alex is 18 years old (over 16), Child Benefit eligibility extends to children under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is in sixth form completing his A-levels, this counts as approved education. You live in the UK, Alex lives with you at home (meaning you're responsible for him), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

You meet all the criteria for Child Benefit for Alex. You can go ahead and make your claim. Just remember that if Alex's circumstances change - for example, if he leaves education, starts working 24 hours or more per week, or starts claiming certain benefits in his own right - this could affect your entitlement, and you would need to inform HMRC.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is currently in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels). Under UK Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16, but eligibility extends to children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criterion as he is under 20 and in sixth form completing his A-levels. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, which means they are responsible for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Although Alex is over 16, he qualifies because he is under 20 and remains in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels). The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.

Under UK Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16, but eligibility extends to children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criterion as he is under 20 and in sixth form completing his A-levels. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, which means they are responsible for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Although Alex is over 16, he qualifies because he is under 20 and remains in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels). The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
